summary: Backend testing of the Strava-like quasi-real-time Garmin pipeline complete. Created /app/backend/tests/test_realtime_sync_pipeline.py with 14 tests covering non-blocking sync, ingestion dedupe, Redis Stream fan-out, feed cache, since filter, activity-signal, per-user cooldown, global concurrency counter, queue health and status. All 14/14 pass. Prior standalone suites (test_sync_scheduler, test_reliable_queue, test_queue_health) still pass in their native runner (they use custom async runners; not pytest-native so 'pytest' collection errors are expected and not regressions).

minor.issue: db.workouts.update_one filter uses only {id: workout['id']} without user_id. Two users with the SAME Garmin external_id would overwrite each other's derived workout. Observed in Redis stream during test run: multiple synthetic users (mfa_p2, qa1, default) ingested external_id=23473184085 and only ONE workout doc survived. Not a blocker for user_id='default' feature scope, but a real multi-tenant correctness bug worth fixing (filter should be {id: workout['id'], user_id: user_id} OR keying id as garmin-{user_id}-{ext_id}).

If Redis feed cache has FEWER items than the requested `limit` (e.g. cache warmed by an earlier limit=1 call, then a later limit=20 call), the endpoint still returns source=cache with < limit items instead of falling back to DB. Observed: cache had 1 entry, subsequent GET with limit=50 returned only 1 activity. Consider: if len(cached) < limit AND cache < FEED_MAXLEN, fall through to DB + re-warm.

Log line at 20:43:01 shows 'loop error: Connection closed by server.' then silent. The loop catches Exception and continues, relying on redis.asyncio auto-reconnect. This worked (216 events processed post-restart) but there's no explicit reconnect/re-ensure_group on connection loss. If the group is dropped, worker would silently no-op.

action_items: Consider fixing event_worker.handle_event to key workouts by {id, user_id} to prevent cross-user overwrite when external_ids collide (observed in this environment).
Consider a fallback in GET /api/garmin/activities: if cache has < requested limit, hit DB & re-warm, so partial/undersized cache does not degrade response size.
critical_code_review_comments: SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS is respected: sync-worker only writes garmin_activities + emits ACTIVITY_CREATED; event-worker builds workouts + feed cache. Verified.
Idempotency: garmin_activities upsert is keyed on {user_id, external_id} — re-sync did NOT duplicate rows (count stable across two consecutive syncs).
activity-signal is truly read-only: no queue push, no gccli call, only sets cardiocoach:active_signal:{uid} in Redis with TTL. Verified.
Cooldown key cardiocoach:sync_cooldown:{uid} correctly set with TTL <= 900s after a successful sync (verified TTL=899s).
Global concurrency cap counter cardiocoach:garmin:global_active exists, bounded to 8, decremented on release. Verified.
POST /api/garmin/sync returns in < 1s (measured ~0.4s) — non-blocking as required.
Response contract of GET /api/garmin/activities remains backward compatible ({activities, count}) with the additional `source` field. Verified.
test_credentials: user_id=default (gccli session pre-authenticated, no login required)
seed_data_creation: None — used the existing pre-connected gccli Garmin session and default user's real activity history.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New test file /app/backend/tests/test_realtime_sync_pipeline.py is the canonical regression suite for the Strava-like pipeline. It uses /app/bin/redis-cli with LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/app/lib to inspect Redis state independently of the app's python-redis client. The default user's garmin_activities already had 31 rows before testing; re-sync did NOT create duplicates. Feed cache and workouts populate only for NEW ingested activities (dedupe suppresses events on re-ingestion), so a fresh 'default' sync in an environment where activities already exist will not emit new ACTIVITY_CREATED events — this is by design and correctly tested. The known cross-user workout overwrite (see backend_issues.minor) surfaces only when different user_ids share an external_id, which happened here due to prior load-test data.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional blockers found. Two minor design observations (workouts overwrite key, undersized-cache fallback) are documented as action_items for the main agent.
